message:
docs(fp-frame): add crate-level rustdoc (br-9s0g / fd90.65)

Continuation of fd90.61-64 doc-gap audit. fp-frame is the largest
workspace crate (64K lines) and the user-facing analytics surface
(DataFrame, Series, GroupBy, Rolling/Expanding/EWM, Resample,
accessors). The docs.rs landing page rendered empty.

Add a //! module doc covering:
  - Architecture: glue layer over fp-types (scalars / dtypes /
    nan*), fp-columnar (Column storage), fp-index (Index +
    alignment), fp-runtime (optional decision policy).
  - Core data structures: DataFrame (columnar table), Series
    (single-column labeled vector), FrameError.
  - GroupBy and window operations: SeriesGroupBy + DataFrame-side
    wrappers, Rolling / Expanding / Ewm + DataFrame variants,
    Resample / DataFrameResample.
  - Accessors mirroring pandas .str / .dt / .cat / .sparse:
    StringAccessor, DatetimeAccessor (+ TzLocalizeOptions /
    TzAmbiguousPolicy / TzNonexistentPolicy), CategoricalAccessor
    + CategoricalMetadata, SparseAccessor.
  - Type coercion (pd.to_*): to_numeric, to_datetime family
    (+ ToDatetimeOptions / ToDatetimeOrigin), to_timedelta family
    (+ ToTimedeltaOptions / ToTimedeltaErrors).
  - Frame combination: concat_dataframes / concat_series variants
    matching pd.concat axis/join/keys/ignore_index. Pointer to
    sibling fp-join crate for merge-style joins.
  - SQL IO via fp-io's DataFrameIoExt extension trait.

Pure documentation, no code change. cargo doc -p fp-frame
--no-deps builds cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' (all
intra-doc links resolve via existing use statements). 1433 tests
pass; clippy -D warnings clean; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-9my2","title":"pandas/io/excel: read_excel(sheet_name=[...]) selected-sheet dict parity","description":"py signature:
pandas.read_excel(io, sheet_name=["Sheet1", "Sheet2"], header=0, index_col=None, skiprows=0) -> dict[str, DataFrame]

Current fp-io only supports one sheet name. Add pandas parity for list-like sheet selection returning a dict keyed by requested sheet names in request order.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:31.631840663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:41:24.038044576Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:41:24.036013633Z","close_reason":"read_excel_sheets + read_excel_sheets_bytes with per-sheet dict return landed in commit d071612 (co-landed by another agent's 5tz7 Excel patch); 4 unit tests pass covering all-sheets, subset, unknown, and path-vs-bytes parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9p0u","title":"fp-types Timestamp struct + Timedelta arithmetic (4r56 Phase 2)","description":"Phase 2 of 4r56 (Timedelta arithmetic shipped in commit 68e2087).

**Scope.** Add a `Timestamp` struct to fp-types providing:
- Nanosecond-precision i64 storage (Unix epoch anchored).
- NaT sentinel (i64::MIN) for missingness.
- Arithmetic: `Timestamp + Timedelta -> Timestamp`, `Timestamp - Timestamp -> Timedelta`, `Timestamp - Timedelta -> Timestamp`.
- Saturation on overflow; NaT propagation.
- Serde round-trip + semantic_eq.

**Non-scope (deferred to Phase 3).**
- TZ-aware Timestamp: a `tz: Option<String>` field is reserved now, but TZ-dependent arithmetic (DST transitions, tz conversion via chrono_tz) lands in a separate slice that pulls chrono_tz into fp-types's dep graph.
- ISO 8601 parsing / strftime display: chrono integration stays in downstream crates; Phase 2 ships constructor-from-nanos only.
- Display: Phase 2 uses a debug-ish format `"Timestamp[<nanos>, UTC]"`; pandas notation lands in Phase 3.

**Blast radius.** Purely additive in fp-types (no existing call-site touched). Zero dep-graph change (no chrono in fp-types yet).

**Tests (10):**
- Construction from nanos.
- Timestamp + Timedelta arithmetic.
- Timestamp - Timestamp = Timedelta.
- Timestamp - Timedelta arithmetic.
- NaT propagation (Timestamp or Timedelta is NaT).
- Saturation on overflow.
- semantic_eq NaT-aware.
- Ordering (non-NaT comparison respects chronological order).
- Serde round-trip.
- Compile-time Send+Sync assertion.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:23:53.821472519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:26:20.881490680Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:26:20.879041374Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","review","timestamp"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9q98","title":"[HIGH] REVIEW: mixed naive/offset to_datetime utc parity drift","description":"Found while building frankenpandas-ow57 datetime conformance matrix.

Failing input: series_to_datetime, datetime_utc=true, values=["2024-01-15 10:30:00", "2024-01-15 10:30:00+05:30"].

Expected from live pandas oracle: ["2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00", null].

Actual from FrankenPandas: ["2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00", "2024-01-15 05:00:00+00:00"].

Impact: pandas inferred-format coercion rejects the offset-aware second value in this mixed naive/offset sequence, while FrankenPandas accepts and normalizes it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T06:06:41.985402560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:41:32.596622149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:41:32.594733021Z","close_reason":"Fixed to_datetime mixed naive/offset UTC parity drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datetime","parity","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-9s0g","title":"fd90.65: fp-frame needs crate-level rustdoc summarizing the user-facing surface","description":"Continuation of fd90.61-64 doc audit. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-frame - the largest workspace crate (64K lines) and most user-facing one. Public surface includes DataFrame, Series, GroupBy, Rolling/Expanding/EWM, Resample, accessors (Categorical/Sparse/String/Datetime), to_datetime/to_timedelta/to_numeric coercion fns, concat_*, plus the merge/join helpers. Doc summarizes the major user-facing types organized by area: core types, group-by/window operations, accessors, type coercion, frame combination. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:59:39.250764334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:02:38.261550285Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:02:38.257565231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-9uuq","title":"fp-conformance: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling std|var","description":"Add live pandas oracle coverage for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling(window).std() and var() with ddof=1 semantics, interleaved groups, and windows that become invalid due to missing values. Log parity artifacts under phase2c so failures show exact bucket-local drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T05:29:38.729141183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:40:51.995865875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:40:51.993469499Z","close_reason":"shipped 6edb388: 2 fp-frame tests (groupby_rolling_std_var_int64_input_widens_to_float_with_ddof1_semantics, groupby_rolling_std_var_all_nan_window_emits_nan) complementary to x5oy's float-input baseline. Locks Int64->Float64 dtype coercion + ddof=1 sample-variance numerics + all-NaN window NaN-emission. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}

@@ -1,6 +1,86 @@
 #![forbid(unsafe_code)]
 #![warn(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]
 
+//! DataFrame / Series core for **frankenpandas** — the user-facing
+//! analytics surface, mirroring pandas' DataFrame and Series APIs
+//! plus their groupby / window / resample / accessor families.
+//!
+//! This is the largest workspace crate. It glues together:
+//! - **fp-types**: scalar values, dtypes, `nan*` reductions
+//! - **fp-columnar**: per-column storage ([`Column`])
+//! - **fp-index**: row labels and binary-op alignment
+//! - **fp-runtime**: optional decision-recording policy
+//!
+//! Most callers reach the contents of this crate via the
+//! `frankenpandas` umbrella prelude (`use frankenpandas::prelude::*`)
+//! or via direct imports (`use fp_frame::DataFrame`).
+//!
+//! ## Core data structures
+//!
+//! - [`DataFrame`]: columnar table with a row [`Index`] and an
+//!   ordered column map. Constructors mirror pandas:
+//!   `from_dict`, `from_series`, `from_columns`, `from_records`.
+//!   Operations cover selection, mutation, reindexing, grouping,
+//!   window-functions, joins, IO round-trips (via fp-io).
+//! - [`Series`]: single-column labeled vector. Built-in arithmetic,
+//!   reductions, and accessor families (string / datetime /
+//!   categorical / sparse).
+//! - [`FrameError`]: failure modes (column-not-found, length
+//!   mismatch, dtype mismatch, alignment error, ...).
+//!
+//! ## GroupBy and window operations
+//!
+//! - [`SeriesGroupBy`] (and the corresponding DataFrame-side
+//!   wrappers): pandas `.groupby()` semantics including aggregation,
+//!   transformation, filtering, and `apply`.
+//! - [`Rolling`] / [`Expanding`] / [`Ewm`]: window-function
+//!   builders for Series (rolling, expanding, exponentially-weighted
+//!   moving stats).
+//! - [`DataFrameRolling`] / [`DataFrameExpanding`] /
+//!   [`DataFrameEwm`]: column-wise variants for DataFrame inputs.
+//! - [`Resample`] / [`DataFrameResample`]: pandas
+//!   `.resample(freq)` for time-indexed Series / DataFrames.
+//!
+//! ## Accessors
+//!
+//! Mirror pandas `.str` / `.dt` / `.cat` / `.sparse` namespaces:
+//!
+//! - [`StringAccessor`]: vectorized string ops on Utf8 Series.
+//! - [`DatetimeAccessor`]: per-element datetime field extraction.
+//!   See also [`TzLocalizeOptions`] / [`TzAmbiguousPolicy`] /
+//!   [`TzNonexistentPolicy`] for tz-aware localization.
+//! - [`CategoricalAccessor`] + [`CategoricalMetadata`]: ordered
+//!   / unordered categorical type machinery.
+//! - [`SparseAccessor`]: paired with fp-columnar's `SparseColumn`.
+//!
+//! ## Type coercion (pandas `pd.to_*` analogs)
+//!
+//! - [`to_numeric`]: best-effort string -> Int64/Float64 with
+//!   `errors='raise'|'coerce'` semantics.
+//! - [`to_datetime`] / [`to_datetime_with_format`] /
+//!   [`to_datetime_with_unit`] / [`to_datetime_with_options`]:
+//!   parse a Series of dates with explicit format / unit / origin
+//!   ([`ToDatetimeOrigin`]) controls. Options bundle:
+//!   [`ToDatetimeOptions`].
+//! - [`to_timedelta`] / [`to_timedelta_with_unit`]: parse
+//!   ISO-8601 / pandas-shorthand durations.
+//!   [`ToTimedeltaOptions`] / [`ToTimedeltaErrors`] for tunables.
+//!
+//! ## Frame combination
+//!
+//! `concat_dataframes`, `concat_series`, `concat_dataframes_with_*`
+//! variants mirror `pd.concat(..., axis=, join=, ignore_index=,
+//! keys=)`. Merge / join helpers live in the sibling **fp-join**
+//! crate (`merge_dataframes_on`, `merge_asof`, `merge_ordered`,
+//! `join_series`).
+//!
+//! ## SQL IO
+//!
+//! Read / write IO for DataFrames lives in **fp-io**, which exposes
+//! the [`DataFrameIoExt`](https://docs.rs/fp-io/latest/fp_io/trait.DataFrameIoExt.html)
+//! extension trait so users can call `df.to_parquet(path)`,
+//! `df.to_sql(conn, ..)`, etc. directly on values from this crate.
+
 use std::{
     cmp::Ordering,
     collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet, HashMap, HashSet},
